clusters.title: Marine Le Pen appeal verdict: Why Tuesday's ruling matters for France
clusters.neutral_headline: Marine Le Pen Faces Key Ruling In France

items.title: Marine Le Pen appeal verdict: Why Tuesday's ruling matters for France
items.standfirst: The leader of France's National Rally leads the opinion polls ahead of the 2027 presidential election and will now find out if she can stand.
items.content: The leader of France's National Rally leads the opinion polls ahead of the 2027 presidential election and will now find out if she can stand.

items.title: Live: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen faces key ruling in race for president
items.standfirst: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen faces a key ruling Tuesday with a Paris appeals court ruling on an EU parliament funds misuse case, which will determine if she can run in the 2027 presidential elections. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.
items.content: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen faces a key ruling Tuesday with a Paris appeals court ruling on an EU parliament funds misuse case, which will determine if she can run in the 2027 presidential elections. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

items.title: Marine Le Pen’s political future at stake with ruling on electoral ban imminent
items.standfirst: Leader of France’s far-right National Rally and a contender for the presidency set to hear appeal decision on TuesdayMarine Le Pen, France’s far-right figurehead and a leading contender for its presidency, will learn on Tuesday whether she can run in next year’s election when a Paris appeals court rules on her attempt to overturn a ban on holding elected office.The ruling will determine whether the far-right National Rally (RN) candidate to succeed the outgoing president, Emmanuel Macron, will be the veteran Le Pen, 57, or her youthful protege, Jordan Bardella, 30. Continue reading...
items.content: Leader of France’s far-right National Rally and a contender for the presidency set to hear appeal decision on TuesdayMarine Le Pen, France’s far-right figurehead and a leading contender for its presidency, will learn on Tuesday whether she can run in next year’s election when a Paris appeals court rules on her attempt to overturn a ban on holding elected office.The ruling will determine whether the far-right National Rally (RN) candidate to succeed the outgoing president, Emmanuel Macron, will be the veteran Le Pen, 57, or her youthful protege, Jordan Bardella, 30. Continue reading...

ai_summary: Marine Le Pen appeals EU parliament funds misuse case
Ruling determines her eligibility for 2027 presidential election
Le Pen leads opinion polls ahead of the election
Paris appeals court decides on electoral ban
Outcome affects France's National Rally party

items.title: China test-launches a ballistic missile in the South Pacific and raises regional concerns
items.standfirst: The launch, using a dummy warhead, took place the same day Australia and Fiji signed a mutual defense treaty meant to counter Chinese influence in the Pacific.
items.content: The launch, using a dummy warhead, took place the same day Australia and Fiji signed a mutual defense treaty meant to counter Chinese influence in the Pacific.(Image credit: Li Xiangchao)

items.title: U.S. says China's nuclear weapons buildup "of great concern" after missile test

items.content: China's military test-launched a long-range ballistic missile from a nuclear-powered submarine in the South Pacific, drawing protest and concern from countries in the region.

items.title: China conducts rare long-range missile test, rattling U.S. allies
items.standfirst: The strategic missile was fired into the Pacific from a nuclear-powered submarine, state media reported, in the Chinese military’s first such test since 2024.
items.content: The strategic missile was fired into the Pacific from a nuclear-powered submarine, state media reported, in the Chinese military’s first such test since 2024.

items.title: World Cup: Spain knock out Portugal and Cristiano Ronaldo

items.content: Spain have qualified for the World Cup quarter-finals after beating Portugal (1-0). Cristiano Ronaldo has played his final World Cup match.

items.content: Cristiano Ronaldo left the pitch for the last time at a World Cup, after Spain beat Portugal 1-0.

items.content: BBC Sport looks at the end of Cristiano Ronaldo's World Cup career as he fails to win football's biggest prize.

items.title: Watch: Spain into quarter-finals after Merino's late winner against Portugal
items.standfirst: Spain leave it late as super-sub Mikel Merino scores a stoppage-time winner to send his side into the quarter-finals, ending Cristiano Ronaldo's hopes of World Cup glory.
items.content: Spain leave it late as super-sub Mikel Merino scores a stoppage-time winner to send his side into the quarter-finals, ending Cristiano Ronaldo's hopes of World Cup glory.

items.title: Merino strikes late as Spain edge Portugal, drawing curtain on Ronaldo's World Cup career
items.standfirst: Substitute Mikel Merino scored an injury-time winner as European champions Spain beat Iberian rivals Portugal in a tense last-16 encounter to book a place in the World Cup quarter-finals and put an end to Cristiano Ronaldo's career on football's biggest stage.
items.content: Substitute Mikel Merino scored an injury-time winner as European champions Spain beat Iberian rivals Portugal in a tense last-16 encounter to book a place in the World Cup quarter-finals and put an end to Cristiano Ronaldo's career on football's biggest stage.

items.title: Portugal vs Spain: Merino scores in injury time to send La Roja to quarter-finals
items.standfirst: Substitute ​Mikel Merino scored in ​added time as Spain edged Portugal 1-0 to reach the World Cup quarter-finals on Monday, extending La Roja's run without conceding ​a goal and putting an end to Cristiano Ronaldo's career on football's biggest stage. Read our blog to see how the match unfolded.
items.content: Substitute ​Mikel Merino scored in ​added time as Spain edged Portugal 1-0 to reach the World Cup quarter-finals on Monday, extending La Roja's run without conceding ​a goal and putting an end to Cristiano Ronaldo's career on football's biggest stage. Read our blog to see how the match unfolded.

items.content: France captain Kylian Mbappe has condemned racist comments from a Paraguayan senator.

items.title: Kylian Mbappé condemns Paraguayan senator over 'despicable' racist abuse
items.standfirst: Kylian Mbappé condemned a Paraguayan senator on Monday for making racist comments about him after France's World Cup last-16 victory over Paraguay, calling her "despicable" and "not worthy" of her position. The remarks sparked widespread condemnation from French officials, including President Emmanuel Macron.
items.content: Kylian Mbappé condemned a Paraguayan senator on Monday for making racist comments about him after France's World Cup last-16 victory over Paraguay, calling her "despicable" and "not worthy" of her position. The remarks sparked widespread condemnation from French officials, including President Emmanuel Macron.

items.title: Mbappe condemns racist posts from Paraguay senator

items.content: France forward Kylian Mbappe condemns a Paraguayan senator as "despicable" and unworthy of her position after she made a number of racist remarks mocking his origin and education.

ai_summary: Kylian Mbappe condemns racist abuse from senator

clusters.title: Student loan promotion in England and Wales amounted to mis-selling, MPs say
clusters.neutral_headline: UK Government Accused Of Mis Selling Student Loans

items.title: Student loan promotion in England and Wales amounted to mis-selling, MPs say
items.standfirst: Treasury select committee also says ministers have moral obligation to reverse last year’s repayment threshold freezeSlideshows that compared student loan repayments with the cost of a mobile phone contract, and YouTube videos that did not mention the fact that loan terms could change amounted to mis-selling by the government, MPs have said.The chancellor, Rachel Reeves, caused a furore last year when she announced that the repayment threshold on plan 2 student loans would be frozen at £29,385 for three years from April 2027. Continue reading...
items.content: Treasury select committee also says ministers have moral obligation to reverse last year’s repayment threshold freezeSlideshows that compared student loan repayments with the cost of a mobile phone contract, and YouTube videos that did not mention the fact that loan terms could change amounted to mis-selling by the government, MPs have said.The chancellor, Rachel Reeves, caused a furore last year when she announced that the repayment threshold on plan 2 student loans would be frozen at £29,385 for three years from April 2027. Continue reading...

items.title: Phone contract comparisons 'amounted to mis-selling' student loans, MPs say
items.standfirst: A new report says students were not well-enough informed that their loan terms could change retrospectively.
items.content: A new report says students were not well-enough informed that their loan terms could change retrospectively.

ai_summary: MPs say student loan promotion was misleading
Government compared loan repayments to phone contracts
Loan terms could change retrospectively
MPs criticize government's handling of student loans
Chancellor Rachel Reeves faces criticism

clusters.title: Party Rock Anthem singer Lauren Bennett dies aged 37

items.title: Party Rock Anthem singer Lauren Bennett dies aged 37
items.standfirst: Bennett's cause of death was not immediately clear and bandmates from her former group G.R.L say their "hearts are broken".
items.content: Bennett's cause of death was not immediately clear and bandmates from her former group G.R.L say their "hearts are broken".

items.title: Lauren Bennett, singer on LMFAO’s Party Rock Anthem, dies aged 37
items.standfirst: British-born vocalist competed on The X-Factor before joining the girl groups Paradiso Girls and G.R.L.Lauren Bennett, member of the girl group G.R.L. and featured artist on LMFAO’s global smash hit Party Rock Anthem, has died at the age of 37.“It is with great sadness that we share the passing of our beloved Lauren,” the group wrote on their Instagram page. “Our hearts are broken, and we cannot begin to express how much she meant to us.” A cause of death was not specified. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Russia launches deadly attacks on Ukraine ahead of NATO summit
clusters.neutral_headline: Russia Launches Deadly Attacks On Ukraine

items.title: Russia launches deadly attacks on Ukraine ahead of NATO summit
items.standfirst: At least 22 people were killed in Russian strikes on Ukraine and Ukraine's president is asking for more Patriot air defenses. NBC News' Keir Simmons reports.
items.content: At least 22 people were killed in Russian strikes on Ukraine and Ukraine's president is asking for more Patriot air defenses. NBC News' Keir Simmons reports.

items.title: Russian missile and drone strikes kill 24 in Kyiv ahead of NATO summit
items.standfirst: Russia launched a deadly barrage of missiles and drones on Kyiv on Monday, killing at least 24 people and wounding more than 100 in its second major strike on the capital in a week. The attack came on the eve of a NATO summit, where President Volodymyr Zelensky is expected to press allies for stronger air defence support.
items.content: Russia launched a deadly barrage of missiles and drones on Kyiv on Monday, killing at least 24 people and wounding more than 100 in its second major strike on the capital in a week. The attack came on the eve of a NATO summit, where President Volodymyr Zelensky is expected to press allies for stronger air defence support.

items.title: Deadly Russian strikes hit Kyiv on eve of Trump trip to NATO summit
items.standfirst: The attack, which Ukraine’s president had warned of hours earlier, comes days after a combined Russian assault killed at least 31 people in Kyiv.
items.content: The attack, which Ukraine’s president had warned of hours earlier, comes days after a combined Russian assault killed at least 31 people in Kyiv.

ai_summary: Russia launches missile and drone strikes on Kyiv
At least 24 people killed and over 100 injured
Attack occurs ahead of NATO summit
Ukraine's president asks for more air defense support

items.content: Ezra Jin Mingri, who founded Beijing’s Zion Church, was detained in October in one of China’s largest crackdowns on a single church in decades.
